title: recon JSON output
description: Stable v2.0 contract for the single-domain success output of `recon <domain> --json`. This root object describes exactly one output mode: a successful single-domain lookup. The other output modes have their own shapes, documented in $defs and in docs/schema.md. `recon batch <file> --json` (default) emits a bare array of records, one per input domain (see $defs/BatchArray); each element is either a single-domain success object (this root) or a $defs/BatchErrorRecord. `recon batch <file> --json --include-ecosystem` always wraps that array in a $defs/BatchResult object; on an all-failed batch the wrapper is still emitted, with the error records under `domains` and an empty `ecosystem_hyperedges`. `recon batch <file> --ndjson` emits one $defs/BatchNdjsonRecord per line. `recon delta <domain>` emits a $defs/DeltaReport. The dataclass in recon_tool.models.TenantInfo and the formatter in recon_tool.formatter.format_tenant_dict are the implementation source of truth. Consumers should ignore unknown properties; additive changes are non-breaking within 2.x. A few top-level fields are emitted only in specific modes or behind specific flags and are intentionally absent from `required`; each such property states its condition in its own description. See tests/test_json_schema_contract.py for the executable single-domain contract and tests/test_batch_ndjson_schema.py for the batch-record rule set.

tenant_id.description: Microsoft 365 tenant UUID. Null for non-M365 domains.

display_name.description: Organization display name. Falls back to the queried domain when no better signal is available.

default_domain.description: Primary domain within the tenant. Falls back to the queried domain when no tenant was detected.

queried_domain.description: The registrable apex (eTLD+1) recon analyzed, after validating and normalizing the input. A pasted URL or sub-host is reduced to its apex (e.g. mail.acme.co.uk -> acme.co.uk); recon --exact keeps the literal host.

provider.description: One-line provider summary (e.g. 'Microsoft 365 (primary) via Proofpoint gateway + Google Workspace (secondary)').

confidence.description: Overall confidence in the merged result.

region.description: Geographic region when detectable via OIDC (e.g. 'NA', 'EU', 'WW').

google_idp_name.description: Third-party IdP name for Google Workspace when detectable.

likely_primary_email_provider.description: Inferred primary (hedged) when MX is a gateway and DKIM/TXT/OIDC points to a specific downstream.

email_gateway.description: MX-detected email security gateway (Proofpoint, Mimecast, etc.).

sources.description: Source names that successfully contributed to the result.

partial.description: True when any core source is in degraded_sources (CT-only degradation does not flip this flag).

degraded_sources.description: Source names that failed or were unavailable.

slugs.description: Stable fingerprint slugs for programmatic matching.

detection_scores.description: Per-slug detection score level aggregated from evidence.

insights.description: Derived intelligence lines. Exact wording may evolve; presence and signal types are stable.

domain_count.description: Number of domains in the tenant.

tenant_domains.description: All domains found in the tenant.

related_domains.description: Domains inferred from CNAME breadcrumbs, CT logs, or autodiscover delegation.

email_security_score.description: Count of the 5 email controls present: DMARC reject/quarantine, DKIM selectors, SPF strict (-all), MTA-STS, BIMI.

dmarc_policy.description: DMARC policy when a DMARC record is present.

site_verification_tokens.description: TXT tokens observed on the apex (e.g. 'google-site-verification=', 'MS=').

ct_provider_used.description: Best-effort CT-pipeline telemetry, not part of the guaranteed contract (removed from the required set in the v2.0 schema-hardening pass so the CT pipeline can evolve). Which CT provider answered, or '(cached)' suffix when data came from the per-domain CT cache.

ct_subdomain_count.description: Number of subdomains returned after filtering.

ct_cache_age_days.description: Best-effort CT-pipeline telemetry, not part of the guaranteed contract (optional since the v2.0 schema-hardening pass). Age of the CT cache entry in days when cached data was used. Null when data came from a live provider.

ct_attempt_outcome.description: Best-effort CT-pipeline telemetry, not part of the guaranteed contract (optional since the v2.0 schema-hardening pass; the enum may gain values as the pipeline evolves). v1.9.25: outcome of the CT enumeration attempt. cache_hit when the cache-first short-circuit served the result. live_success when a live provider returned data. live_rate_limited when a provider hit HTTP 429 or local pacing / wait-ceiling limits and no provider or cache returned data. breaker_open when every failed provider was stopped by an open local breaker before a useful live attempt. live_other_failure for non-429 errors (timeout, 5xx, JSON parse). cache_miss when providers returned empty-but-not-error and no cache entry existed. skipped when --no-ct was passed. Null on older records or sources that do not touch CT.

cert_summary.description: Certificate transparency summary. Null when no CT data was obtained.

cloud_instance.description: Microsoft cloud-sovereignty host from M365 OIDC discovery. Microsoft controls this value and may add sovereign clouds, so it is an open string rather than a closed enum; known values include microsoftonline.com (commercial), microsoftonline.us (US Gov), and partner.microsoftonline.cn (China).

lexical_observations.description: Hedged observations from CT subdomain lexical taxonomy.

bimi_identity.description: BIMI VMC identity. Null when no BIMI VMC was observed.

fusion_enabled.description: True when the Bayesian fusion layer ran for this lookup (under --fusion, or default-on from v2.0). Disambiguates the fusion arrays: when false, slug_confidences and posterior_observations are empty because fusion was not computed; when true, an empty array means fusion ran and found nothing.

schema_version.description: The recon-schema contract version this record conforms to (SH7, v2.0+). Lets a detached payload (in a log, queue, or tool result, separated from this schema's $id) be routed across a future 2.x to 3.0 boundary.

record_type.description: Self-describing discriminator for the output mode (SH7, v2.0+). 'lookup' on a single-domain success object; the other object-shaped modes carry 'batch_result' (the --include-ecosystem wrapper), 'delta' (a delta report), or 'error' (a batch error record). A consumer can branch on this one field instead of inferring the shape from which keys are present.

slug_confidences.description: Bayesian per-slug posterior means (stable v2.0+). An object mapping each detected slug to its posterior mean in [0, 1], parallel to detection_scores. Populated only when fusion runs (see fusion_enabled); empty object otherwise. The Beta layer operates on raw evidence; the network layer (posterior_observations) propagates through chained claims.

posterior_observations.description: Marginal posteriors over high-level claims from the Bayesian network at recon_tool/data/bayesian_network.yaml (v1.9; stable v2.0+). Each entry includes the posterior P(node=present | E) and the central 80% credible interval. Populated only when fusion runs (see fusion_enabled); empty array otherwise. The Beta layer (slug_confidences) operates on raw evidence; this network layer propagates through chained claims.

surface_attributions.description: Per-subdomain attribution to a SaaS or infrastructure provider, derived from CNAME-chain classification of related_domains. Each entry maps one subdomain to its primary service. Application-tier matches (Auth0, Shopify, Zendesk, ...) take precedence over infrastructure-tier matches (Cloudflare, Fastly, CloudFront, ...) when a chain produces both — the infrastructure layer is recorded in infra_slug / infra_name as supplementary context. Empty array when no related domains classified.

evidence_conflicts.description: Top-level array of cross-source disagreements (v1.7+). Each entry names a merged field where 2+ sources provided different non-None values, with every candidate preserved. Empty array when sources agreed. The conflict-aware merger picks a winner using source-confidence ordering; this array surfaces the alternates so consumers can see what was discarded. The legacy `conflicts` dict that --explain emits remains for backwards compatibility.

chain_motifs.description: CNAME chain motifs (v1.7+). Each entry records an ordered proxy/CDN/origin shape (e.g. Cloudflare → AWS origin, Akamai → Azure origin) that fired on a related subdomain's CNAME chain. Observable structure only — never an ownership claim. Empty array when no motif matched any related subdomain. The motif catalog lives in recon_tool/data/motifs.yaml and can be extended via ~/.recon/motifs.yaml (additive only).

infrastructure_clusters.description: Per-domain CT co-occurrence community detection report (v1.8+). Always emitted. ``algorithm`` records which path produced the partition (``louvain`` for small graphs, ``connected_components`` when the graph exceeds the size cap, ``skipped`` when no graph could be built). Modularity is 0.0 in the fallback / skipped paths. Members within each cluster are sorted; clusters are sorted by size descending. Observable structure only — clusters describe co-issuance, not ownership.

fingerprint_metadata.description: Per-slug relationship metadata (v1.8+). Object keyed by detected fingerprint slug. Each value is `{product_family, parent_vendor, bimi_org}` — descriptive hints sourced from the fingerprint YAML. Slugs without any populated metadata are omitted entirely. Empty object when no detected slug carries metadata. Drives the v1.8 ecosystem hypergraph; never an ownership assertion.

unclassified_cname_chains.description: Conditional. Present only when ``--include-unclassified`` is passed, absent otherwise; consumers should not treat it as always present. CNAME chains the surface classifier resolved but could not attribute to any cname_target fingerprint. Each entry: {subdomain, chain: [hop1, hop2, terminal]}. Wildcard-DNS echoes are filtered out before this list is populated. Feeds the fingerprint-discovery loop in validation/find_gaps.py and the /recon-fingerprint-triage Claude skill.

evidence.description: Conditional. Present only with --explain, absent otherwise; consumers should not treat it as always present. Per-detection evidence trace.

explanation_dag.description: Conditional. Present only with --explain on the lookup_tenant MCP tool, absent otherwise; consumers should not treat it as always present. Top-level keys: evidence, slugs, rules, signals, insights, each a list of records with id and references.

shared_verification_tokens.description: Conditional, batch-only. Present on a per-domain success record in `recon batch --json` output only when two or more domains in the batch shared a TXT verification token. Never emitted in single-domain --json or in --ndjson (which streams before the batch-wide pass runs). Each entry pairs a shared token with the peer domain that also carried it.

shared_tenant.description: Conditional, batch-only. Present on a per-domain success record in `recon batch --json` output only when two or more domains in the batch shared an M365 tenant_id. Never emitted in single-domain --json or in --ndjson. Each entry carries the shared tenant_id and the peer domains under it.

shared_display_name.description: Conditional, batch-only. Present on a per-domain success record in `recon batch --json` output only when two or more domains in the batch normalized to the same display name. Hedged signal (customer-supplied brand text, not cryptographic). Never emitted in single-domain --json or in --ndjson. Each entry carries the raw display_name, its normalized form, and the peer domains.

wildcard_sibling_clusters.description: Stable v1.7+ (reshaped to objects in v2.0). Each entry is an object {names: [...]} whose `names` is the deduplicated, sorted list of non-wildcard SANs from a single cert that also covered at least one wildcard SAN. The object envelope leaves room for a future per-cluster attribute (the generating wildcard SAN or issuer). Empty array when CT data showed no wildcard certs or none had concrete siblings to harvest. Bounded: at most 10 clusters, 20 names per cluster.

deployment_bursts.description: Stable v1.7+. Cohorts of cert issuances clustered by `not_before` proximity (default 60s window, min 3 distinct non-wildcard SANs). Output is intentionally relative — span_seconds + name list — and never claims ownership. Empty array when no cluster meets the minimum size. Bounded — at most 8 bursts, 25 names per burst.

window_start.description: ISO-8601 UTC of earliest not_before in this burst.

window_end.description: ISO-8601 UTC of latest not_before in this burst.

names.description: Distinct non-wildcard SANs co-issued inside this window, sorted.

source_type.description: DNS or HTTP source class (TXT, MX, CNAME, NS, SRV, CAA, SPF, HTTP).

SurfaceAttribution.description: Maps one subdomain to its primary service from CNAME chain classification. primary_* fields are the application-tier match when present, else the most specific infrastructure-tier match. infra_* fields are populated only when both an application and an infrastructure tier matched — they record the CDN or load balancer fronting the application service.

ChainMotif.description: One CNAME chain motif observed against a related subdomain. ``chain`` is the ordered subset of hops that matched the motif's markers — not the full original chain.

subdomain.description: Related subdomain whose chain produced the match.

chain.description: Matched hops in order, one per motif marker.

InfrastructureClusterReport.description: Stable v1.8+. Wraps the cluster list with the algorithm path and the underlying graph metrics so downstream tools can interpret the partition correctly.

algorithm.description: Path that produced the partition. ``connected_components`` is the deterministic fallback used when the graph exceeds the v1.8 node cap; ``skipped`` means no graph was built (no CT entries, fewer than two nodes, or no edges).

modularity.description: Newman-Girvan modularity for the Louvain partition. 0.0 in the fallback / skipped paths.

partition_stability.description: Partition consensus across a Louvain seed sweep: the mean pairwise adjusted Rand index between the partitions produced by stability_runs different seeds (CAL11). 1.0 means every seed produced the identical partition; lower values flag partition degeneracy that a single modularity score cannot see. null outside the Louvain path (fallback / skipped), where the partition is deterministic and the measure is not applicable. Added 2.2.0; schema-additive.

stability_runs.description: Number of distinct Louvain seeds in the partition-stability sweep. 0 when partition_stability is null. Added 2.2.0; schema-additive.

node_count.description: Distinct non-wildcard SAN hostnames observed across the cert entries.

edge_count.description: Edges in the co-occurrence graph (each edge weights co-issuance count).

EcosystemHyperedge.description: One observed multi-domain signature in a ``recon batch --json --include-ecosystem`` run (v1.8+). Surfaced as the top-level ``ecosystem_hyperedges`` array of the batch wrapper, where the per-domain entries appear under ``domains``. Not emitted in single-domain ``--json`` output.

edge_type.description: Rule that produced the edge: shared CT top issuer, shared BIMI VMC organization, shared fingerprint parent_vendor, or shared fingerprint-slug pair (≥2 overlap).

key.description: Value the rule fired on. Issuer name, BIMI org, parent vendor, or — for ``shared_slugs`` — the comma-joined intersection of overlapping slug names.

members.description: Sorted apex domains that share the signature.

FingerprintMetadata.description: Relationship hints for one fingerprint slug. Stable v1.8+. Each field is optional — at least one will be non-null when this record appears.

parent_vendor.description: Corporate parent the product rolls up to (e.g. "Microsoft", "Salesforce" for Slack).

bimi_org.description: Exact organization name expected on a BIMI VMC for domains under this product.

InfrastructureCluster.description: One community in the per-domain SAN co-occurrence graph. Stable v1.8+.

cluster_id.description: Stable id within this report (largest cluster is 0).

members.description: SAN hostnames in this cluster, sorted.

shared_cert_count.description: Number of cert-entry contributions across edges fully inside this cluster (not unique certs).

dominant_issuer.description: Most common issuer name across contributing certs.

EvidenceConflict.description: A single field where two or more sources provided different non-None values during merge. Stable v1.7+.

field.description: Merged field name. One of: display_name, auth_type, region, tenant_id, dmarc_policy, google_auth_type.

UnclassifiedCnameChain.description: A CNAME chain captured during surface classification that did not match any cname_target fingerprint. Surfaced only with ``--include-unclassified``. ``chain`` is ordered: each hop is a CNAME target, terminal last. Useful for proposing new fingerprints — the terminal hostname is usually a third-party SaaS endpoint we don't yet recognize, an intra-org CDN, or noise.

PosteriorObservation.description: Per-node Bayesian posterior with an 80% credible interval (v1.9; stable v2.0+). The interval is evidence-responsive: it widens monotonically as evidence thins and narrows as it accumulates (a construction property; it is not a frequentist coverage interval, see correlation.md section 4.4). ``sparse=true`` flags the passive-observation ceiling for a node whose absence carries no disconfirming weight; a declarative node (one whose absent public-declaration signals are themselves evidence, CAL14) can be confidently-absent, with a low posterior, a narrow interval, and ``sparse=false``. ``conflict_provenance`` (v1.9.1+) carries the source-pair detail behind any n_eff penalty.

interval_low.description: Lower bound of the 80% credible interval (evidence-responsive, not frequentist coverage).

interval_high.description: Upper bound of the 80% credible interval (evidence-responsive, not frequentist coverage).

evidence_used.description: Bound observations that fired for this node, formatted as ``slug:<name>`` or ``signal:<name>``.

n_eff.description: Effective sample size used to derive the interval; lower means wider interval.

sparse.description: True when n_eff is at the floor and absence carried no disconfirming weight for this node; the interval reflects the passive-observation ceiling, not strong belief. For a declarative node (CAL14) absence is evidence, so a confidently-absent declarative node has n_eff above the floor and ``sparse=false`` despite a low posterior.

conflict_provenance.description: Cross-source disagreements that contributed to this node's n_eff penalty (v1.9.1+). Empty array when no conflicts dampened the interval. Surfaces the source-pair detail dropped by the legacy count-only path; pairs with the existing top-level ``evidence_conflicts`` array.

evidence_ranked.description: Fired bindings ranked by absolute log-likelihood-ratio contribution, descending (v1.9.3.2+; the contributing set, one per correlation group). Empty when no bindings fired, and omitted on batch records (the batch shape predates it).

entropy_reduction_nats.description: This node's share of the information recovered: H(prior marginal) - H(posterior) in nats, signed (negative when evidence widens the node). Per-node breakdown of the top-level entropy-reduction total (CAL10). Added 2.2.0; schema-additive.

unit_counterfactuals.description: Exact leave-one-unit-out counterfactuals for every evidence unit informative for this node in this run, sorted by absolute delta descending. An evidence counterfactual over the model, never a causal claim about the world. Deltas are individually exact but not additive (units interact through the DAG). Added 2.2.0; schema-additive.

NodeEvidence.description: One fired binding's quantified influence on a node's posterior (v1.9.3.2+). llr is the natural-log likelihood ratio (positive favours present); influence_pct normalizes |llr| to a percentage across the node's fired bindings.

name.description: Slug or signal name that fired.

llr.description: Natural-log likelihood ratio for the fired observation.

influence_pct.description: |llr| as a share of the node's total fired |llr|, in percent.

NodeUnitCounterfactual.description: One evidence unit's exact leave-one-out influence on a node's posterior (added 2.2.0). The engine re-runs exact inference with the unit masked as structurally unobserved, globally across the DAG, so posterior_without reflects everything else still observed - including support flowing from other nodes' evidence through the CPTs.

unit.description: Evidence-unit name: a correlation-group name (e.g. m365_indicators, dmarc_policy) or an ungrouped binding's slug/signal name.

kind.description: Unit kind: a correlation group, or the ungrouped binding's kind.

observed.description: Whether the unit fired, or is an informative absence on a declarative node (there delta is typically negative: the absence drags the posterior down).

posterior_without.description: P(node=present | evidence with this unit masked as structurally unobserved).

delta.description: posterior - posterior_without: positive when the unit pushes the node up. Individually exact, not additive across units.

NodeConflict.description: One cross-source disagreement carried through to a NodePosterior (v1.9.1+). ``field`` is the merged TenantInfo field whose sources disagreed; ``sources`` lists the distinct sources that contributed candidate values; ``magnitude`` is the n_eff penalty (in n_eff units) this conflict applied to the node.

field.description: Merged field name. One of: display_name, auth_type, region, tenant_id, dmarc_policy, google_auth_type.

sources.description: Distinct source identifiers that contributed a candidate value for this field.

magnitude.description: n_eff penalty this single conflict applied. Uniform at v1.9.1; exposed as a number so future per-node relevance weighting is schema-additive.

DeltaReport.description: Output shape of `recon delta <domain>` and `recon <domain> --compare <file>`. Stable.

record_type.description: Record discriminator (SH7, v2.0+); always 'delta' on this shape.

BatchErrorRecord.description: Emitted in place of a success object when a single domain in a batch run fails. Appears as an array element in ``recon batch --json`` (BatchArray), as a ``domains`` element in the ``--include-ecosystem`` BatchResult wrapper, and as a line in ``recon batch --ndjson``. A domain yields this shape when it fails input validation (e.g. a malformed apex) or when its lookup raises. Exactly four keys (domain, error, error_kind, record_type) and no overlap with the single-domain success contract (which requires dozens of fields), so a consumer can branch on the key set alone. The deterministic classifier in recon_tool.schema_contract.classify_batch_record encodes that rule.

domain.description: The raw domain string from the input list, before validation and lowercasing.

error.description: Human-readable failure reason: the validation message or the lookup error text.

error_kind.description: Machine-readable error category (SH8, v2.0+) so a consumer can route or alert on a code rather than the free-text message. validation = the domain failed input validation; timeout = the lookup timed out; lookup = any other lookup failure. The enum may gain values.

record_type.description: Record discriminator (SH7, v2.0+); always 'error' on this shape.

BatchArray.description: Default shape of ``recon batch <file> --json`` (without --include-ecosystem): a bare JSON array, one element per input domain, in input-file order. Each element is either a single-domain success object (the root schema of this file) or a BatchErrorRecord. Success elements may additionally carry the batch-only cross-domain fields (shared_verification_tokens, shared_tenant, shared_display_name) when the batch surfaced a peer relationship.

BatchNdjsonRecord.description: One line of ``recon batch <file> --ndjson`` output. Same per-record shape as a BatchArray element: either a single-domain success object (the root schema of this file) or a BatchErrorRecord. NDJSON streams per domain as each completes, so the cross-domain batch enrichment fields (shared_verification_tokens, shared_tenant, shared_display_name) and the --include-ecosystem hyperedge envelope are never present in this mode; both require the full result set before emission.

BatchResult.description: Shape of ``recon batch <file> --json --include-ecosystem``: a wrapper object carrying the per-domain results under ``domains`` and the cross-domain ``ecosystem_hyperedges`` array as a sibling. Without --include-ecosystem the batch JSON is a bare BatchArray instead, with no wrapper. From v2.0 (SH9) the wrapper is always emitted when --include-ecosystem is set, including on an all-failed batch (domains carries the error records, ecosystem_hyperedges is empty), so the top-level type does not flip to a bare array. Stable v2.0.

record_type.description: Record discriminator (SH7, v2.0+); always 'batch_result' on this wrapper.

domains.description: Per-domain results, one entry per input apex in input-file order. Each entry is either a single-domain success object (the root schema of this file) or a BatchErrorRecord.

ecosystem_hyperedges.description: Multi-domain signatures observed across the batch. Always present in this wrapper (empty array when no rule fired). Each entry is one ``EcosystemHyperedge``.
